title: humboltia lamellaris
intro: Humboldtia lamellaris, also known as the umbrella tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. Humboldtia lamellaris is prepared into various herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including skin infections and respiratory problems.
